title: JT1: Prisma Schema & Relational Foundation
description:
Translate the tutorial's Mongoose/MongoDB data modeling (Part 4, Video 1:54:00-2:11:00) into Epicflow's PostgreSQL relational architecture using Prisma.

SUB-ISSUES:
[JT1.1] Define Core Models: Create JobBoard, JobColumn, and JobApplication in schema.prisma. Replace MongoDB ObjectId arrays with standard PostgreSQL foreign key relations (boardId, columnId) using CUIDs.
[JT1.2] Array and Default Types: Translate Mongoose tags: [String] to PostgreSQL tags String[]. Set default status to 'applied' and order to 0.
[JT1.3] Bi-Directional Cascade: Configure onDelete: Cascade so deleting a user wipes their board, deleting a board wipes columns, deleting a column wipes jobs.
[JT1.4] Workflow Traceability (Epicflow Specific): Add optional workflowId to JobApplication with onDelete: SetNull to track jobs auto-generated by the FlowX engine.
[JT1.5] Migration: Run npx prisma migrate dev --name init_job_tracker to apply the relational schema.

KEY FILES: prisma/schema.prisma
MODELS: JobBoard (id, name, userId -> User, columns[]), JobColumn (id, name, order, boardId -> JobBoard, jobs[]), JobApplication (id, company, position, location?, salary?, jobUrl?, status='applied', tags String[], description?, notes?, order=0, columnId -> JobColumn, userId, workflowId? -> Workflow)
INDEXES: @@index([userId]) on JobBoard, @@index([boardId]) on JobColumn, @@index([columnId]) and @@index([userId]) on JobApplication
notes:
TECH-STACK ADAPTATION (Bead plan said Prisma, actual stack is Drizzle):
- ORM: Drizzle ORM (not Prisma). Used pgTable() definitions with relations() for relational queries.
- PKs: integer().primaryKey().generatedAlwaysAsIdentity() matching existing usersTable pattern (not CUIDs).
- FKs: .references(() => table.id, { onDelete: 'cascade' }) for cascade deletes.
- Tags: text().array().notNull().default([]) for PostgreSQL text[] (not String[]).
- workflowId: varchar({length: 255}) nullable (no Workflow model exists yet, so no FK constraint — will be added when Workflow model lands).
- Auth: Clerk (not Better Auth). JT2 will need to hook into Clerk webhook or provider instead of databaseHooks.
- Migration: drizzle-kit generate + drizzle-kit migrate (not prisma migrate dev).
- Migration file: drizzle/0002_shallow_bill_hollister.sql — applied successfully to Neon PostgreSQL.
close_reason: Schema implemented in Drizzle ORM. 3 tables (job_boards, job_columns, job_applications) with cascade FKs and relations. Migration 0002 applied to Neon.

title: JT2: Auth Hooks & Default Board Initialization
description:
Replicate the tutorial's initUserBoard.ts logic (Video 2:12:00-2:25:00) that runs after sign-up, adapting it to Epicflow's Better Auth implementation.

SUB-ISSUES:
[JT2.1] Create Init Server Action: Build initializeEpicflowJobBoard in src/lib/actions/job-board.ts. Use Prisma's nested create syntax to generate the 5 default columns (Wishlist order:0, Applied order:1, Interviewing order:2, Offer order:3, Rejected order:4) simultaneously with the board named 'Automated Job Hunt'. Check for existing board first to prevent duplicates.
[JT2.2] Hook into Better Auth: Modify src/lib/auth/auth.ts. Add a databaseHooks.user.create.after hook to automatically fire initializeEpicflowJobBoard(user.id) when a new user registers.
[JT2.3] Seed Script (Optional): Replicate the tutorial's seed.ts script using Prisma instead of Mongoose to inject mock job applications for local testing.

KEY FILES: src/lib/actions/job-board.ts, src/lib/auth/auth.ts
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-1 (Prisma schema must exist first)
notes:
TECH-STACK ADAPTATION (Bead plan said Better Auth, actual stack is Clerk):
- Server Action: src/lib/actions/job-board.ts with initializeJobBoard(userId: number).
- Drizzle insert (not Prisma nested create). Board insert + batch column insert in sequence.
- Idempotent: checks for existing board before creating. Safe to call from multiple paths.
- Hook 1 (Primary): Clerk webhook at app/api/clerk-webhook/route.ts — fires on user.created event, creates user row then immediately initializes board.
- Hook 2 (Fallback): POST /api/users at app/api/users/route.ts — client-side provider calls this on login, also initializes board after new user creation.
- Both hooks wrap initializeJobBoard in try/catch so board init failure never blocks user creation.
close_reason: initializeJobBoard server action created. Hooked into Clerk webhook (user.created) and POST /api/users. Idempotent, creates board + 5 default columns per user.

title: JT3a: Epicflow Native Node Configuration
description:
Define the Job Tracker node so it acts as an active component inside the Epicflow visual canvas (FlowX-specific synergy).

SUB-ISSUES:
[JT3a.1] Node Configuration: Create src/lib/integrations/job-tracker.ts. Define the IntegrationConfig with provider: 'job_tracker'.
[JT3a.2] Config Fields: Add UI inputs for Board, Target Column, Company Name, and Position. Ensure placeholders support Epicflow's mustache syntax (e.g., {{gmail.company}}).

KEY FILES: src/lib/integrations/job-tracker.ts
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-1 (schema must exist), Epicflow Bead 16 (Custom Node) and Bead 24 (Execution Engine)

title: JT3b: Workflow Execution Engine Integration
description:
Create the execution engine logic for the Job Tracker node so workflows can automatically create job applications.

SUB-ISSUES:
[JT3b.1] Write Execution Engine Logic: Create executeJobTrackerNode in src/lib/node-executors/job-tracker.ts. Accept nodeId, userId, config, and workflowId parameters.
[JT3b.2] Handle Order Math: Query Prisma for the target column's highest order value (findFirst with orderBy desc), add 100, and assign it to the new JobApplication to push it to the bottom of the list.
[JT3b.3] Record Creation: Use db.jobApplication.create to persist the new record, linking it to the workflowId for traceability. Handle tag string splitting (comma-separated to String[]).

KEY FILES: src/lib/node-executors/job-tracker.ts
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-1 (schema), deeppivot-3 (node config)

title: JT4: Dashboard Shell & Kanban UI with Shadcn Dialogs
description:
Build the KanBan shell, columns, and the Shadcn Dialog forms for creating/editing jobs (Tutorial Video 2:26:00-3:09:00).

SUB-ISSUES:
[JT4.1] Route & Protection: Create src/app/dashboard/job-tracker/page.tsx. Use await auth.api.getSession() to protect the route and fetch the user's board data with columns and jobs included.
[JT4.2] Kanban Board Component: Create KanbanBoard.tsx (Client Component). Render a grid of columns mapping the tutorial's COLOR_MAP to the column names.
[JT4.3] Job Application Card UI: Build JobApplicationCard.tsx. Render a Shadcn Card displaying position, company, tags (as pills), and the external job URL link.
[JT4.4] Create/Edit Dialog Forms: Build CreateJobDialog.tsx and EditJobDialog.tsx. Replicate the grid layout for inputs (Company/Position side-by-side, Location/Salary side-by-side) using Shadcn dialog, input, textarea components.
[JT4.5] Dropdown Menus: Implement the vertical dots dropdown on the Job Card for 'Edit', 'Move To [Column]', and 'Delete' actions using Shadcn dropdown-menu.

KEY FILES: src/app/dashboard/job-tracker/page.tsx, src/components/job-tracker/KanbanBoard.tsx, JobApplicationCard.tsx, CreateJobDialog.tsx, EditJobDialog.tsx
SHADCN DEPS: dialog, card, dropdown-menu, input, textarea, badge
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-1 (schema must exist for types)

title: JT5: Job Tracker Server Actions & Data Mutations
description:
Replace the tutorial's API routes and Mongoose calls with Next.js Server Actions using Prisma (Tutorial Video 3:09:00-4:39:00).

SUB-ISSUES:
[JT5.1] Create Server Action: In src/lib/actions/job-applications.ts, create createJobApplication(data). Validate session via Better Auth, calculate the order (+100 to the last item in the target column via Prisma findFirst orderBy desc), use Prisma create, and call revalidatePath('/dashboard/job-tracker').
[JT5.2] Update Server Action: Create updateJobApplication(id, data). Handle tag string splitting (comma-separated -> String[]) and general data patching via Prisma update. Verify user ownership before updating.
[JT5.3] Delete Server Action: Create deleteJobApplication(id). Verify the user owns the record before executing db.jobApplication.delete(). Revalidate path after deletion.
[JT5.4] Client Form Binding: Wire the handleSubmit functions in the Create/Edit Dialogs to call these Server Actions and handle loading/error states with useTransition or useState.

KEY FILES: src/lib/actions/job-applications.ts
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-1 (schema), deeppivot-5 (UI forms to bind to)

title: JT6: Drag & Drop Architecture with dnd-kit
description:
Implement the complex column-and-card drag-and-drop mechanics shown in the tutorial (Video 4:39:00-5:23:00).

SUB-ISSUES:
[JT6.1] Install Dependencies: npm install @dnd-kit/core @dnd-kit/utilities @dnd-kit/sortable
[JT6.2] Context Providers: Wrap the Kanban grid in DndContext and SortableContext. Configure closestCorners collision detection and PointerSensor with an 8px activation constraint.
[JT6.3] Sortable Hooks: Implement useDroppable on the column containers and useSortable on the Job Cards. Apply CSS transform utilities for smooth drag animations.
[JT6.4] Active States & Overlays: Create the activeId state. Render a DragOverlay component with 50% opacity to prevent layout shifting during movement.
[JT6.5] Drag End Algorithm (Critical): Implement handleDragEnd. Calculate target vs source indices. Apply the mathematical algorithm (multiply index by 100 to leave spacing) to determine the new order integer without rewriting the entire array.
[JT6.6] Optimistic UI & Persist: Update the local React state immediately on drop, then trigger the updateJobApplication Server Action in the background to persist the new columnId and order to PostgreSQL.

PACKAGE DEPS: @dnd-kit/core, @dnd-kit/utilities, @dnd-kit/sortable
DEPENDS ON: deeppivot-5 (Kanban UI), deeppivot-6 (Server Actions for persistence)

title: JT7: End-to-End Testing & Synergy Review
description:
Finalize the integration and ensure it works perfectly within the Epicflow production environment.

SUB-ISSUES:
[JT7.1] Workflow Trigger Testing: Create a test workflow in Epicflow ('Webhook Trigger' -> 'Add to Job Board'). Fire the webhook and confirm the job appears in the Kanban board immediately upon refresh.
[JT7.2] UI Synergy Check: Ensure the kanban board's dark mode styling matches the global CSS variables defined in Epicflow Bead 02 theme system. Verify all Shadcn components respect the theme.
[JT7.3] Build Verification: Run npm run build to catch any lingering TypeScript errors inherited from the complex dnd-kit event types.

VALIDATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] User sign-up successfully creates the initial Prisma records (board + 5 columns)
- [ ] The Epicflow canvas allows dropping the Job Tracker node
- [ ] Workflows successfully execute and create JobApplications
- [ ] Drag-and-drop kanban mechanics function smoothly with optimistic UI updates
- [ ] Dark mode styling is consistent with Epicflow theme

DEPENDS ON: All previous JT issues (deeppivot-1 through deeppivot-7)
